problem_spec.name: spaceship_titanic
problem_spec.description: Binary classification: predict whether a passenger was Transported to an alternate dimension. Dataset: Spaceship Titanic (Kaggle), 8693 training samples, 13 features. Features: PassengerId (group_num), HomePlanet (Earth/Europa/Mars), CryoSleep (bool), Cabin (deck/num/side format), Destination (3 planets), Age (float), VIP (bool), 5 spending columns (RoomService, FoodCourt, ShoppingMall, Spa, VRDeck), Name. Target: Transported (bool). ~50/50 class balance. Key feature engineering opportunities: cabin parsing (deck/side strongly predictive), group features from PassengerId (family traveling together), spending patterns (CryoSleep passengers have zero spend), age binning, interaction features. Current baseline: F1=0.7914 (logistic regression, basic features). Top Kaggle scores: ~0.81 accuracy. Use 5-fold stratified CV.
constraints: Report mean F1 and accuracy across folds
No test set leakage (test.csv is held out for Kaggle submission)
problem_spec.data_description: CSV with columns: PassengerId, HomePlanet, CryoSleep, Cabin, Destination, Age, VIP, RoomService, FoodCourt, ShoppingMall, Spa, VRDeck, Name, Transported. Missing values present in most columns. Cabin format: Deck/Num/Side. PassengerId format: GroupNum_PersonNum (people in same group are traveling together).
forbidden_patterns: Do not use test.csv for training or validation
Do not use Name as a direct feature (data leakage risk)
